BRAND NEW VEHICLE.  PURCHASED ON 10/30/2020. FRONT COLLISION KEEPS FLASHING A RED LIGHT IN THE TOP OF DASH THAT SHOWS UP IN WINDSHIELD.  THIS HAPPENS WITH NO OTHER VEHICLES ON THE ROAD AT RANDOM TIMES.  WHEN THE VEHICLE NEEDS TO STOP OR WARN YOU IT WILL NOT.  IT WILL JUST LET YOU

narrative.headline: 2020 GMC Acadia: electrical and drivetrain lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 92 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 28%, followed closely by the engine (24%) and power train (21%); steering (15%) and forward collision avoidance (9%) round out the top five. No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported. Only 2 complaints involved a crash, one each in the electrical and forward collision avoidance areas.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 26 reports (28%), with 1 tied to a crash. No fires or injuries reported.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 22 reports (24%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.sample: WHEN THE VEHICLE IS ALREADY IN MOTION, IT AUTOMATICALLY SHIFTS OUT OF DRIVE AND I AM LEFT IN THE MIDDLE OF THE ROAD UNABLE TO ACCELERATE FOR A FEW SECONDS UNTIL THE VEHICLE AUTOMATICALLY SHIFTS BACK TO DRIVE. THIS HAPPENS AFTER PULLING OUT OF MY GRAVEL DRIVEWAY WHICH IS A SLIGHT
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 19 reports (21%), close behind the engine. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.sample: My steering assist is reduced drive with care message is on. The AC is no longer working and the fan is constantly running and sounds horrible. The temperature gauge has not come off of cold. After researching this issue online-it looks like this is a constant problem with Acadia
issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 14 reports (15%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.blurb: The smallest of the top five: 8 reports (9%), with 1 tied to a crash. No fires or injuries reported.
